sections.subtitle: Match Overview
sections.text: Paris Saint-Germain (PSG), sitting at the top of the league with 78 points, hosts Nice, who are in 4th place with 57 points, in Round 31 at the iconic Parc des Princes in Paris. This matchup promises to be an intriguing battle between a dominant force and a competitive top-tier side. The referee, Willy Delajod, averages 4.0 yellow cards per game and 0.29 red cards per game, which could influence the physicality of the encounter.

sections.subtitle: Recent Head-to-Head History
sections.text: In their last meeting on October 6, 2024, Nice hosted PSG and the game ended in a 1-1 draw, with Ousmane Dembélé earning the Player of the Match title with an impressive Sofascore rating of 8.3. Over the last 5 matchups between these teams, PSG has secured 2 wins, Nice has 1 win, and there was 1 draw, showing a relatively balanced rivalry. Historically, both teams have scored in 5 of the last 6 encounters, suggesting a high likelihood of goals from both sides in this game as well.

sections.subtitle: PSG's Dominance and Key Players
sections.text: PSG leads the league in total goals scored with 85, ranking 1st, and also boasts the best defense with only 33 goals conceded. Their attacking prowess is led by Ousmane Dembélé, who tops the league with 21 goals and holds a league-best average Sofascore rating of 7.81. Bradley Barcola is another standout, ranking 1st in assists with 10 and 5th in goals with 14. Achraf Hakimi, with a league rank of 2 in average rating at 7.8, adds defensive stability and attacking flair with 6 assists. Meanwhile, Fabián Ruiz has been a standout against Nice historically, averaging a Sofascore rating of 7.76 with 2 goals and 2 assists in the last 5 matchups.

sections.subtitle: Nice's Strengths and Key Performers
sections.text: Nice comes into the game with a solid attacking output, often scoring early as shown by their streak of being the first to score in 8 of their last 10 matches. Leading their attack is Evann Guessand with 11 goals and 7 assists, supported by Gaëtan Laborde who has contributed 9 goals. Jonathan Clauss has been in form recently, averaging a Sofascore rating of 7.66 over the last 5 matches and providing 3 assists. Defensively, Youssouf Ndayishimiye adds grit with an average rating of 7.15, while Tom Louchet stands out with a league rank of 2 in aerial duels won at 78.6%.

sections.subtitle: Team Streaks and Tactical Insights
sections.text: PSG has failed to keep a clean sheet in their last 4 games, while Nice has gone 7 games without one, indicating defensive vulnerabilities on both sides. Both teams have a strong trend of scoring, with PSG seeing over 2.5 goals in 6 of their last 8 matches and Nice in 4 of their last 5. Additionally, both teams have scored in 7 of PSG’s last 8 games and all of Nice’s last 5, pointing to an open, goal-heavy contest. PSG dominates possession with a league-best 68.44% average, while Nice will likely rely on counter-attacks, a tactic that has worked for them in recent head-to-heads.

sections.subtitle: In-Form Players to Watch
sections.text: For PSG, Ousmane Dembélé is in exceptional form, averaging a Sofascore rating of 8.08 over the last 5 matches with 2 goals and 3 assists. Désiré Doué is another player to watch, averaging 7.82 with 3 goals in the same period. On the Nice side, Jonathan Clauss and Hicham Boudaoui have been pivotal, with ratings of 7.66 and 7.6 respectively over their last 5 games. These players could be game-changers in crucial moments.

sections.subtitle: Statistical Highlights and Expectations
sections.text: PSG leads the league in corners per game with an average of 6.47, while also creating the most big chances at 172, ranking 1st. However, they’ve missed 107 big chances, ranking 18th for conversion efficiency at 37.79%. Nice tends to score heavily between the 31st and 45th minutes, with 23.08% of their goals in that period, while PSG peaks between the 46th and 60th minutes with 30.3% of their goals. Expect PSG to dominate possession and create numerous chances, while Nice might exploit defensive lapses late in halves.

sections.subtitle: Disciplinary and Set-Piece Trends
sections.text: PSG has the fewest yellow cards in the league with only 38, averaging 1.19 per game, and both teams have seen less than 4.5 cards in most recent encounters, with 5 of 7 head-to-heads under this threshold. In terms of corners, PSG’s high average contrasts with a head-to-head trend of less than 10.5 corners in 6 of their last 8 meetings against Nice. With referee Willy Delajod’s average of 4.0 yellow cards per game, expect a moderately physical game without excessive bookings.
